title: elaeocarpus teysmannii subsp. rhizophorus
intro: Elaeocarpus teysmannii subsp. rhizophorus, also known as the Rhizophore Elaeocarpus, is a plant species belonging to the Elaeocarpaceae family, native to Indonesia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus teysmannii subsp. rhizophorus include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat various skin conditions, such as wounds, burns, and eczema. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for its medicinal uses, and its leaves, bark, and roots are commonly used in herbal preparations.
